title: Crown Castle closes sale of fiber and small cell units
summary: Crown Castle has closed the sale of its fiber business to Zayo Group and its small cell business to Arium Networks. #pressrelease

title: Inseego goes global with Nokia FWA buy
summary: Inseego CEO Juho Sarvikas discusses deal to buy Nokia's FWA business that will see it double revenues, expand beyond the US and enter the consumer CPE market.

title: Cable One seeing inconsistent competition from Starlink
summary: Cable One is seeing haphazard competition from Starlink, as pricing and offers have varied from market to market, but views satellite broadband as a 'formidable competitor,' says CEO Jim Holanda.

title: Ruckus sale could lead to more cable tech consolidation
summary: Vistance Networks, the former CommScope, is selling its Ruckus unit to Belden for $1.84 billion. Vistance will retain its cable and PON unit, Aurora Networks, and look to grow it via possible M&A.

title: The Buildout: Michigan WOWed by more fiber connections
summary: This week in broadband builds: WideOpenWest (WOW) connects 17,000 more locations in Michigan; Comcast comes to Bloomsburg, Pennsylvania; Arvig to extend Minnesota fiber lines; New York names its BEAD winners – and more.

title: Eurobites: Turbulence continues for VMO2 as revenue slips 6.5%
summary: Also in today's EMEA regional roundup: Proximus and friends collaborate on Flanders fiber; Eutelsat, Station Satcom reach maritime deal; retrans ruling from EU court.

title: Samsung Networks has one of its worst quarters since 5G was born
summary: Revenues fall one quarter at Samsung's networks business, which blames 'headwinds' and says 'cost discipline' is needed.

title: Predictability of telco cloud spend still a 'touchy subject'
summary: Execs from Vodafone Intelligent Services and Hrvatski Telekom shed light on some of the cloud challenges faced by telcos, and the common mistakes that are made when deploying the tech.

title: Global smartphone shipments exceed expectations with 1% growth in 1Q26, but second-half outlook remains uncertain – Omdia
summary: Persistent inflation has compressed household discretionary budgets, creating a widening gap between channel sell-in and underlying sell-out. #pressrelease

title: 22% of European telcos active in D2D satellite as market moves to early commercialization – Omdia
summary: European operators are largely converging on D2D solutions that enable unmodified smartphones to connect directly to satellites using existing mobile technologies. #pressrelease

title: SCTE launches first 'microcredential,' offers new PNM course
summary: SCTE has launched its first 'microcredential' – focused on fiber blowing skills – and a new, modular course series on Proactive Network Maintenance (PNM). #pressrelease

title: Samsung posts bumper Q1 but warns of tighter memory supply
summary: Samsung Electronics has posted another record quarter as sales and earnings soared, but warns that memory supply will likely worsen.

title: Vistance to sell Ruckus unit to Belden for $1.84B
summary: Vistance Networks (formerly CommScope) said it has struck a deal to sell its Ruckus Networks business to Belden for $1.84 billion in cash. #pressrelease

title: Testing Ericsson v. 1Finity in AT&T's open RAN
summary: Ericsson and 1Finity small cell radios in AT&T's network went head-to-head in a benchmark performance test from Signals Research Group, preceding further rollouts later this year.

title: Render Networks broadens vision beyond FTTH
summary: Equipped with new funding and the acquisition of mPower Innovations, Render is expanding its customer base and positioning itself as an end-to-end system for critical infrastructure builds, according to CEO Stephen Rose.

title: South Korea invests $49M in AI research to transform broadcasting industry
summary: The initiative, spearheaded by the Korea Communications Commission, will help the industry respond to challenges resulting from changes in media consumption patterns.

title: Eurobites: Telefónica, Sateliot combine on 5G IoT
summary: Also in today's EMEA regional roundup: Nokia sells FWA unit to Inseego; APFN brings in new boss; Openreach veteran turns up at Lightning Fibre.

title: AI-mad Verizon to continue with cuts after CEO's jobs warning
summary: Verizon CEO Dan Schulman has now completed the 13,000 layoffs he promised last November, but more could lie ahead.

title: Brazil's Nicnet taps Aprecomm to optimize its fiber gateways
summary: Nicnet, a fiber operator in Brazil, is deploying Aprecomm's AI-assisted customer experience and customer premises equipment management tech, including its Wi-Fi optimization software, on broadband gateways. #pressrelease

title: Optimum eyes virtualization to bridge the FTTP-HFC reliability gap
summary: Optimum has teamed with Harmonic for its first large-scale deployment of a virtual CMTS. 'Virtualization could level the playing field' between HFC and FTTP, says COO Nate Edwards.
